name: Algiers Point — Canal Street Ferry Landing
description: The Algiers side of the Canal Street ferry crossing, where the ferry has not run since August 29 but the landing dock still serves as a gathering point for people moving information on foot between the west and east banks.
atmosphericDetails: A handwritten message board nailed to the ferry terminal wall — names, locations, and requests for information, some of them already weathered past legibility
The smell of the river here is different from the flood plane — moving water, less chemical, almost clean by comparison
A woman sitting on the dock edge with a notebook, taking down names from people who approach her — Delphine Arceneaux, Darnell's cousin, the original source of the grid coordinates
